rationale: Both evaluations reach essentially the same conclusion through identical reasoning: strong full-stack/TypeScript/Node breadth, but no Kotlin/Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate and only dated, superficial Java exposure, making the candidate a poor fit for a JVM/Spring IDE role. The 1-point difference (3 vs 4) is within ordinary run-to-run noise and, if anything, slightly favors the renamed variant rather than penalizing it. No concern, score shift, or reasoning in the variant keys off the name change — the strengths and concerns are the same facts reworded.
